creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
(In reply to Alan Somers from comment #1)

Konstantin Belousov replied to me on the CURRENT mailing list (freebsd-current@) and he suspected me to have "options BPF_JITTER" enabled in kernel which is indeed the fact. He refered to  r316767 which caused the problem and suggested to either

a) disable the kernel option
b) set net.bpf_jitter.enable=0 in /etc/sysctl.conf

I did b) and the kernel boots as expected.

I consider this a workaround, not a fix (to the contrary to "FIXED" marking this issue in PR database).

I close the PR.
